channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Aluminum Prices and Aluminum Futures Prices
item.description: Barchart offers updated aluminum futures prices, charts, and related market information. The listing indicates the market is focused on the active July 2026 contract, suggesting near-term trader attention is centered on the front-month future.

item.title: ALUMINIUM Charts and Quotes
item.description: TradingView’s aluminum page includes charting and recent technical commentary pointing to a possible bullish rebound from a key support area. The analysis suggests traders are watching whether buyers can defend the zone and reverse the recent weakness.

item.title: Aluminium faces 'black swan' supply shock, Mercuria says
item.description: Mercuria's top metals analyst says the global aluminium market is facing a 'black swan' event, with Middle East war disruptions triggering a supply shock. The report says this could create a deficit of roughly two million tons by year-end, implying tight supply and possible price pressure.

item.title: Latest Aluminium News
item.description: NewsNow aggregates the latest aluminium headlines, including items on Chinese output above cap due to price incentives, London Metal Exchange trading halts, and production cuts at Bahrain's smelter. Several of the listed stories focus on supply constraints, price strength, and production changes across the aluminium market.

item.title: Aluminum Industry News | Latest Headlines & Updates
item.description: This page compiles recent aluminium industry headlines, including reports on Rio Tinto's first-quarter 2026 production and Iranian aluminium output data. It serves as a running news feed on production, capacity, and broader market developments.

item.title: Aluminium Market Data
item.description: Fastmarkets' aluminium market page highlights recent analysis that metals markets are shifting toward a security-of-supply theme. It also notes active industry developments such as Tsingshan Holding Group exploring another aluminium smelter project in Indonesia.

item.title: Aluminum industries latest news
item.description: AL Circle provides a live news hub covering bauxite, alumina, primary aluminium, secondary aluminium, downstream, and end-user developments. The page is designed as a current feed for aluminium industry news and market updates.

item.title: Aluminum Market Trends
item.description: S&P Global's aluminium market trends page tracks key drivers affecting the US aluminum industry in 2026. It is framed around the company's Aluminum Symposium and focuses on broader market and capacity-related trends.

item.title: Paolo Rocca steps down as Tenaris CEO amid tensions with Milei
item.description: Paolo Rocca is stepping down as chief executive of Tenaris after more than two decades in the role. Gabriel Podskubka, currently COO, is set to take over as CEO while Rocca remains chairman and president of Techint Group.

item.title: News listing | Tenaris
item.description: Tenaris’s newsroom highlights recent company updates, including a leadership change, a geothermal partnership, and an acquisition agreement in Romania. The listing provides links to several current corporate announcements relevant to the company’s steel pipe business.

item.title: Copper futures dropped to near $6.3 per pound as elevated prices discouraged buying activity in China
item.description: Copper futures slipped for a second consecutive session as high prices curbed buying interest in China. Despite the pullback, the outlook for Chinese demand remains resilient, supported by clean energy and technology-related consumption.

item.title: Copper prices surge to record $6.5 per pound amid tight supply and AI-fueled demand
item.description: Copper hit a record high as traders focused on tight supply conditions and growing demand linked to artificial intelligence infrastructure. The article frames the rally as part of a broader bullish trend driven by long-term electrification and technology demand.

item.title: Copper futures price today
item.description: Copper futures were quoted lower, reflecting a pullback from recent highs. Reuters-linked market coverage highlighted supply-side developments, including the planned restart of Codelco’s El Teniente smelter after a collapse-related shutdown.

item.title: Copper market news and forecasts
item.description: Fastmarkets' copper coverage includes several recent supply-side developments, including strong African output from Glencore and lower production at Kamoa-Kakula. The updates underscore how mine performance and sulfuric acid shortages are shaping current copper supply conditions.

item.title: Industry Data
item.description: AISI reports that domestic raw steel production in the U.S. for the week ending May 16, 2026 was 1,898,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 82.2 percent. This indicates stronger recent mill operating rates.

item.title: SteelRadar homepage update featuring steel news and pricing
item.description: SteelRadar’s homepage is dated May 17, 2026 and highlights recent iron and steel industry news and prices. The visible item in the results mentions Metal Park launching 30,000-ton cut-and-bend rebar capacity, which is relevant to steel production capacity, but the snippet does not provide a fuller article excerpt.

item.title: Italy's Prysmian to Buy Encore Wire in $4.2B Deal
item.description: Prysmian announced a major acquisition of Texas-based Encore Wire, a move aimed at strengthening its North American presence. While not limited to the past 24 hours in the provided results, it is a relevant recent business development for the company and cable market.

item.title: Copper: Tariff risk fuels rally
item.description: Copper prices remained supported by trade-policy concerns, with tariff risk contributing to bullish sentiment. The piece suggests market participants are pricing in continued supply and policy uncertainty.

item.title: Aluminium recycling sector flags EU ETS risks, surge in copper prices too
item.description: The article notes that rising copper prices are affecting broader metals and recycling markets. It also connects the move to policy and cost pressures in Europe, where emissions-related risks are influencing industrial inputs.

item.title: News & Insights - Aluminum Market Update
item.description: CRU’s news page includes aluminum market updates such as Hydro’s Q1 results, where higher prices supported margins despite weaker North American extrusion demand. The item is relevant to current production trends, capacity utilization, and regional demand conditions.

item.title: Ball Corporation reports record 2025 results as aluminium sustainability metrics advance
item.description: Ball Corporation says its 2025 annual and sustainability report shows both record financial results and progress on environmental goals. The company reported that 74% of the aluminum used in its global beverage packaging business came from recycled sources in 2025, highlighting ongoing sustainability improvements in aluminum packaging.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 18.05.2026
item.description: SteelRadar’s latest daily news page highlights recent developments in the global iron and steel market. The page aggregates industry updates, pricing news, and commentary, but the snippet available does not show a specific article headline focused on production or capacity changes.

item.title: Tenaris acquires Artrom Steel Tubes for EUR86 million
item.description: Tenaris announced a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Artrom Steel Tubes SA for EUR86 million on a cash-free and debt-free basis. Artrom is a steel tubes company, and the deal is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2026. This is the most relevant recent item tying Tenaris to steel operations in Italy/Europe in the past day.

item.title: Aluminium Price Today
item.description: Investing.com shows aluminum futures at 3,561.65, unchanged from the previous close. The page also references market commentary about Comex aluminum premiums easing on tariff rollback expectations, suggesting tariff policy remains an active driver of pricing.

item.title: Smelter closure to wipe nearly 30% of US aluminum capacity
item.description: Fastmarkets reports that Magnitude 7 Metals planned to curtail operations at its Marston, Missouri smelter because high energy costs made the plant financially unfeasible. The closure would remove a large share of U.S. primary aluminum smelting capacity and leave only four primary smelters operating in the country.

item.title: Steel prices retreat from nine-month highs as seasonal slowdown weighs on demand
item.description: Steel rebar futures fell below CNY 3,200 per ton after reaching nine-month highs, as expectations of a seasonal slowdown in demand and softer downstream consumption pressured the market. Mysteel’s latest monthly report also pointed to weakening supply-demand fundamentals that could keep construction steel prices under pressure in China this month.

item.title: HRC Steel slips from recent highs after strong run
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel prices eased to 1,128.02 USD/T, down from a recent high of 1,143.00 USD/T, though prices remain elevated versus recent periods. The move suggests some cooling after the recent rally in steel prices.

item.title: Copper futures slip to near two-week lows as equities sell off; China demand weighs on prices
item.description: Copper futures fell to around $6.2 per pound, their lowest level in nearly two weeks, as a broader selloff in equities pressured commodity markets. The report also notes that elevated copper prices have discouraged buying activity in China, adding to the weakness.

item.title: Copper Futures Price Today
item.description: Copper futures were last quoted at 6.2530, down from a previous close of 6.3340. The page also highlights Reuters coverage that Chilean miner Codelco's El Teniente smelter is set to restart after a collapse, a development that could affect near-term supply conditions.

item.title: Nickel futures extend losses as softer ore and sulfate prices weigh on sentiment
item.description: Nickel futures traded around $18,500 per tonne, extending losses toward a one-month low. The report says weaker nickel ore prices in the Philippines and easing nickel sulfate prices have reduced near-term support and reinforced expectations of lower production costs across the supply chain.

item.title: Nickel price update: supply disruptions keep bullish momentum intact
item.description: Nickel prices were also reported above $19,500 per tonne, near a two-year high, as supply disruptions and tighter raw material availability continued to support the market. Indonesia's mining quota cuts and a sulfur shortage linked to Strait of Hormuz disruptions added to cost pressures and output constraints.

item.title: HRC Steel Price Climbs to Highest Since December 2023
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel prices rose to 1,135 USD/T on May 19, marking a 0.18% increase from the previous day and reaching the highest level since December 2023. The data also shows steel prices have gained over the past month, reflecting continued upward momentum in the market.

item.title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
item.description: Aluminum prices fell as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump may narrow tariffs on some aluminum and steel products. The move raised expectations that some import levies could be rolled back, pressuring metal prices and weighing on broader industrial metals.

item.title: Nickel rose to 18,957.25 USD/T on May 20, 2026, up 0.94% from the previous day
item.description: Nickel prices continued to strengthen, reaching 18,957.25 USD/T and posting a modest daily gain. The market has also risen 4.45% over the past month and 21.29% over the past year, suggesting a broader upward trend in nickel pricing.

item.title: Nickel rose to 18,702.75 USD/T on May 20, 2026, up 0.23% from the previous day
item.description: A separate Trading Economics update shows nickel trading at 18,702.75 USD/T, reflecting a smaller day-over-day increase. This indicates the market remains elevated and active, with prices holding near recent highs.

item.title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
item.description: Indonesia has reportedly ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, to sharply reduce its ore production quota this year. Bloomberg says the quota could fall to 12 million tons from 42 million tons in 2025, as the country looks to support global nickel prices. The company says it plans to seek a revision.

item.title: Nickel Futures Rebound on Reports of Additional Output Reductions
item.description: Nickel prices recovered after reports of further production cuts, with futures moving back above $18,800 per ton. The move reflects growing attention on supply discipline and its effect on market balances.

item.title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
item.description: Prysmian shares climbed after market commentary suggested the Italian cable maker could benefit from US copper tariffs. The article notes that investors viewed Prysmian as well-positioned in the cable sector amid changing trade conditions.

item.title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Copper futures were trading around $6.28 per pound, with the prior session showing a 2% gain. The update attributes support to improved global risk appetite amid renewed market optimism.

item.title: Nickel Futures Price Today
item.description: Investing.com provides the latest nickel futures quote along with the prior close and trading range information. The page is useful for monitoring short-term market movement and intraday volatility.

item.title: Prysmian | Cables, Energy & Telecom Solutions
item.description: This is Prysmian's official corporate site rather than a news article. It highlights the company's role as a global cable manufacturer and provides background on its energy and telecom solutions business.

item.title: HRC Steel Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel rose to 1,146 USD/T, its highest level since December 2023. The move reflects continued strength in steel pricing over the past several weeks, suggesting firm demand and/or constrained supply.

item.title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
item.description: The article reviews recent U.S. steel market conditions, noting capacity utilization has slipped below the administration’s 80% goal but has shown signs of stabilizing in recent weeks. It also highlights improving weekly production and utilization rates, suggesting potential tailwinds as demand recovers into the second half of the year.

item.title: United States Steel Production
item.description: Trading Economics reports that U.S. steel production rose to 7.2 million tonnes in March 2026 from 6.5 million tonnes in February 2026. The page provides historical context and tracks production trends over time using World Steel Association data.

item.title: Nickel futures recover after reports of additional output reductions
item.description: Nickel prices moved back above $18,800 per ton after reports of further supply reductions, extending a recent rebound from a near four-week low. The move reflects market sensitivity to potential production cuts and tightening supply expectations. The article focuses on pricing, but the driver is directly tied to production and capacity news.

item.title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Aluminum prices rose to 3,650.30 USD/T on May 22, 2026, extending a strong run that lifted the market to its highest level since March 2022. The page also notes a 2.2% gain over the past four weeks and a 45.16% rise over the past 12 months, indicating continued upward momentum despite near-term volatility.

item.title: HRC Steel reaches highest level since December 2023
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel prices climbed to 1,161 USD/T on May 22, 2026, marking the highest level since December 2023. Trading Economics notes that HRC steel has continued to trend higher over the past month and year, signaling persistent upward pressure in the market.

item.title: Steel price rises to 3,171 CNY/T on May 22, 2026
item.description: Trading Economics says steel prices increased to 3,171 CNY/T on May 22, 2026, up slightly from the prior day. The data also shows gains over the past month and year, indicating continued strength in the broader steel market.

item.title: Nickel rose to 18,880 USD/T on May 22, 2026
item.description: Nickel prices edged higher, reaching 18,880 USD/T, up 0.67% from the previous day. The chart and historical data indicate the metal has also risen over the past month and year, suggesting continued strength in the market.

item.title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
item.description: AISI’s latest industry data shows adjusted year-to-date U.S. steel production through May 16, 2026 reached 35,183,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 78.3%. The figures indicate the domestic steel market is running close to the administration’s 80% utilization target.

item.title: Aluminum Market Prices, Data, and Analysis
item.description: S&P Global offers market pricing, analysis, and forward-looking intelligence for aluminum. The page is a market reference for traders and analysts tracking metals trends.

item.title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
item.description: This report says U.S. steel manufacturers are planning more than $6 billion in capital-spending projects that are expected to begin in the near term. The piece points to continued investment in domestic capacity expansion.

item.title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
item.description: This article highlights copper’s growing importance in the energy transition, driven by demand from renewable energy, electric vehicles, and infrastructure. It also notes that producers are responding with more exploration, expansion, sustainability initiatives, and digital innovation.

item.title: What Innovations Are Shaping Global Copper Mining and Processing Today?
item.description: The piece outlines major technologies reshaping copper mining and processing, including automation, artificial intelligence, and green practices. It focuses on how innovation is improving efficiency and sustainability across the industry.

item.title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
item.description: S&P Global projects substantial growth in copper demand through 2040 and discusses the need for more recycling and smarter extraction methods. It also highlights advanced leaching, autonomous haulage, and AI-driven geoscience as key innovations helping the industry meet future demand.

item.title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap.
item.description: The article examines how new technologies and policies may help address an expected copper supply deficit. It covers machine learning for exploration, mine sorting, biomining, and solvent-based extraction methods designed to improve efficiency and reduce environmental impact.

item.title: Copper and the Future: Can New Demand Drivers Replace the Old?
item.description: This article argues that copper is becoming a critical infrastructure metal as new demand drivers such as electrification and technology adoption build on existing industrial demand. It also notes that tariff risks and technological disruptions are part of a shifting market environment.

item.title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
item.description: This article focuses on a bid aimed at solving challenges in the copper value chain to increase sustainable production. It centers on improving copper output while supporting more environmentally responsible practices.

item.title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
item.description: This market commentary says nickel prices have been supported by Indonesia's flat-to-down ore production targets for 2026. It argues that supply discipline in Indonesia, combined with rising EV demand, could push prices toward $20,000 per ton.

item.title: Italy's Tenaris posts surprise 2% rise in sales on stable US, Canada drilling
item.description: Tenaris reported a surprise 2% increase in third-quarter net sales, helped by steady drilling activity in North America that offset weakness in other regions. The company also warned that margins will be affected by the full impact of tariff costs.

item.title: HRC Steel rises to highest level since December 2023 as prices continue climbing
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel increased to 1,162.02 USD/T on May 26, 2026, up 0.34% from the previous day. The broader market trend cited on the page shows continued strength in steel prices over recent weeks and months.

item.title: Steel price falls in Trading Economics market snapshot
item.description: Trading Economics shows steel at 3,161 CNY/T on May 26, 2026, down 0.88% from the previous day. Despite the daily decline, the page notes a modest one-month gain and a stronger year-over-year increase.

item.title: 3 innovations in green steel
item.description: This article highlights several steel-sector sustainability innovations, including Boston Metal’s CO2-free steel process, Kobe Steel’s lower-coke blast furnace technique, and hydrogen-based steelmaking. It also describes H2 Green Steel’s plan to build a large commercial hydrogen steel plant aimed at producing green steel at scale.

item.title: Capstone Copper Reports Record Fourth Quarter 2025 Results
item.description: Capstone Copper reported record fourth-quarter 2025 copper production of 58,273 tonnes, up 8% from the same quarter in 2024. The result was driven primarily by ramping up sulphide production and is relevant to current copper production and capacity trends.

item.title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
item.description: The article focuses on technologies that could help narrow the copper supply-demand gap, including machine-learning-assisted exploration, mine sorting, biomining, and solvometallurgy. It emphasizes that these innovations aim to improve resource efficiency and lower the environmental footprint of copper production.

item.title: Global Nickel Market
item.description: This market update says the IEA estimates global mine nickel supply rose from 3.6 million metric tons in 2023 to 3.9 million metric tons in 2024, nearly a 10% increase. It also notes that the market surplus in 2024 was about 150,000 tons, suggesting the market is relatively tight despite the supply increase.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali to acquire majority stake in its scrap supplier
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali signed a contract to acquire a 65% controlling interest in Com.Steel Inox, its stainless steel scrap supplier. The move is described as upstream integration and is expected to close in the coming months.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali acquires 65 percent of Inox
item.description: The article reports that Cogne Acciai Speciali completed the acquisition of a 65% stake in Com.steel Inox S.p.A. for €24 million. It says the deal supports the company's circularity and upstream integration goals.

item.title: Italy's Cogne Acciai Speciali launches green hydrogen pilot plant
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali has started operations at a green hydrogen pilot plant in Aosta, northern Italy. The project is presented as part of the company’s decarbonization and specialty steel production efforts.

item.title: Steel Market Update: Home
item.description: Steel Market Update reports that sheet and plate price indices increased by $5-20 per short ton from the prior week. This points to a modest rebound in short-term pricing across parts of the steel market.

item.title: Steel sheet and plate price indices rise again
item.description: Steel Market Update says sheet and plate price indices increased by $5 to $20 per short ton from the prior week. The update indicates continued firmness in the U.S. steel market heading into late May.

item.title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
item.description: This video report says aluminum prices are rising because the U.S. premium has reached record highs. It cites supply concerns, three-year-high LME prices, and China’s efforts to prevent overcapacity as key market drivers.

item.title: Copper Prices Crash $0.20! Trade Shock Sends Scrappers ...
item.description: This video says copper prices fell by $0.20 per pound after a new European trade agreement, creating a volatile week for the scrap market. It also references the U.S. COMEX copper price trading around 558 cents per pound.

item.title: Nickel Market Weakens on Oversupply and Soft Demand
item.description: StoneX Senior Metals Analyst Natalie Scott-Gray says nickel prices have lost momentum in 2025 after a strong start. She points to plentiful supply and modest demand over the next two years, which she says could lead to a fifth and sixth consecutive year of surpluses.

item.title: Italian steelmaker Cogne Acciai Speciali sourcing all energy from renewables
item.description: Cogne Acciai Speciali is now sourcing all of its power needs in Europe from renewable energy. The report also says the company plans to build a hydroelectric plant and an electrolyzer at its main site in Aosta.

item.title: HRC steel hits highest level since December 2023 as prices edge lower on the day
item.description: Hot-rolled coil steel increased to 1,143 USD/T, the highest level since December 2023, while the same page also notes a 0.60% day-over-day decline to 1,152.05 USD/T on May 27. The article reflects a market that has been firm over recent weeks but still experiencing normal daily volatility.

item.title: Copper Archives - MINING.COM
item.description: This article appears to cover copper market activity, including trade flows and market tightness linked to tariff-related demand. Based on the search snippet, it is one of the most recent items in the results and is likely relevant to current copper supply conditions.

item.title: AMU Survey: Tightening aluminum market not met by inventory rebuilding
item.description: This market update highlights a tightening aluminum market and notes that inventory rebuilding has not kept pace with supply-demand conditions. It suggests near-term pricing and market balance may remain sensitive to stock levels and broader demand trends.

item.title: Edward Meir's week in review and thoughts for the week of May 25, 2026
item.description: This weekly commentary reviews recent aluminum market developments and discusses the outlook for the week of May 25, 2026. It likely focuses on price direction, market drivers, and supply-demand conditions affecting aluminum trading.

item.title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
item.description: Fox News reports that Century Aluminum is increasing production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs. The segment frames the move as a major boost to U.S. aluminum output, but the provided result is a video clip rather than a full article.

item.title: Steel rises modestly in Chinese market, continuing recent gains
item.description: Steel rose to 3,165 CNY/T on May 28, up 0.25% from the previous day. Trading Economics also reports that steel prices have gained over the past month and are higher versus the prior year, pointing to an overall upward trend.

item.title: Low-Carbon Production of Iron & Steel: Technology Options and Economic Assessment
item.description: This report reviews current iron and steel production and evaluates decarbonization pathways for the sector. It identifies hydrogen, biomass, zero-carbon electricity, and carbon capture and storage as promising options for reducing emissions from steelmaking.

item.title: Op-ed: navigating the steel industry's global transformation through technology and collaboration
item.description: The piece discusses how technology is reshaping the steel industry, with a focus on AI, electric arc furnaces, and hydrogen in the context of climate targets and market pressures. It emphasizes collaboration and operational visibility as key to building a more resilient and sustainable steel sector.

item.title: Chile lowers its copper production outlook amid market and supply concerns
item.description: Economies.com says copper prices edged higher while Chile, the world's largest copper producer, lowered its production outlook. The article connects market pricing with supply-side developments that could affect future production and capacity expectations.

item.title: Copper Futures Price Today - Investing.com
item.description: The page shows copper futures trading at 6.4285, above the previous close of 6.3400. It provides live pricing and market context for recent copper movement.

item.title: This Week's Scrap Metal Prices & Market News
item.description: The update says copper prices remained steady this week, with COMEX copper hovering near recent highs. It also notes that traders are watching global economic data closely for direction.

item.title: Nickel prices, news and market analysis
item.description: Fastmarkets presents a nickel market coverage page focused on prices, news, and market analysis. The page highlights ongoing volatility and dynamics in the nickel market, but the search result does not provide a specific article headline from the last 24 hours.

item.title: Nickel Jul '26 Futures Price
item.description: Barchart provides live nickel futures quotes, charts, technical analysis, and related news. The result indicates market-tracking coverage rather than a standalone news article, but it is useful for assessing short-term price trends.

item.title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
item.description: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, energy-saving nickel extraction method using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon. The process can handle low-grade ores in a single reactor and is reported to cut CO2 emissions by 84% while improving energy efficiency when powered by renewable electricity and green hydrogen.

item.title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
item.description: This article examines how optimizing nickel production can support a more sustainable battery industry and a net-zero future. It emphasizes ESG integration, responsible investment, stakeholder engagement, and stronger ESG reporting as key ways to address the industry's environmental challenges.

item.title: Nickel – a mineral with a challenging role in clean tech
item.description: This piece discusses nickel's growing importance in the clean energy transition and notes that demand is expected to rise significantly this decade. It argues that the industry must scale production with less environmental impact to remain compatible with net-zero goals.

item.title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Trading Economics reports that HRC steel fell to 1,182.11 USD/T on May 29, 2026, down 0.24% from the previous day. It also notes that the broader hot-rolled coil steel price had recently reached 1,143.00 USD/T, the highest level since December 2023, indicating continued volatility in the market.

item.title: AWS to Source Low Carbon Nuton Copper from Rio Tinto for U.S. Data Center Infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services will use the first commercial Nuton bioleached copper from Rio Tinto in U.S. data center infrastructure under a two-year agreement. The collaboration also includes cloud data and analytics support to help optimize the Nuton bioleaching platform as it scales, highlighting lower-carbon and lower-water-intensity copper production.

item.title: Aluminum Facing 'Very Large Supply Hole,' JPM Says
item.description: JPMorgan’s Greg Shearer says the aluminum market is facing a significant supply shortfall and expects prices to remain elevated in the near term. He also discusses how the Middle East conflict is affecting market expectations and supply assumptions.

item.title: Iran Strikes Gulf Aluminum Smelters, Disrupting Global Supply
item.description: This report says strikes in the Gulf have hit major aluminum smelters, shifting the risk from logistics disruption to direct damage to production capacity. It frames the event as a supply shock with broader implications for global aluminum availability.

item.title: Copper braces for another round of US tariff roulette
item.description: Reuters reports that copper markets are reacting to renewed uncertainty around U.S. tariff policy, with traders watching for potential price swings. The story frames tariffs as a key near-term driver of market volatility and sentiment.

item.title: Copper jumps to two-week high after US-Iran truce deal report
item.description: Copper prices rose sharply after reports of a U.S.-Iran truce deal, suggesting a risk-on move in broader commodity markets. The article links geopolitical developments to stronger short-term demand expectations and higher copper prices.

item.title: Copper producers report higher Q1 earnings as prices stay elevated
item.description: Fastmarkets reports that copper producers, including Atlas Mining, posted higher first-quarter 2026 earnings on the back of elevated copper prices. The report also notes changes in concentrate output, making it relevant to both production trends and operating capacity conditions.

item.title: Chile's copper output declines year over year
item.description: Mining.com reports that Chile's copper output fell to 399.954 metric tons from 464.056 metric tons in the same month a year earlier. The piece highlights a significant production drop in the world's largest copper-producing country.

item.title: Copper jumps to a two-week high after truce-related market optimism
item.description: NewsNow aggregates a report that copper rose to a two-week high after news of a U.S.-Iran truce deal. While mainly a price update, it reflects broader market conditions that can influence producer incentives and capacity utilization.
